revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)]][[ja:甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山古墳 (熊谷市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|1=Kumagaya}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.078639|N|139.40675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kabutoyama Kofun''' (甲山古墳, かぶとやまこふん, also known as 大里甲山古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the Ochiyama area. The mound is circular in shape and has been designated as a Prefectural Historic Site by Saitama Prefecture.
== Overview == Kabutoyama Kofun is a large circular Kofun constructed on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Hiki Hills|ja|比企丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiki Hills|1=Hiki Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11547367}} in central Saitama Prefecture. The name "Kabutoyama" (Helmet Hill) is derived from the resemblance of the mound to a kabuto (samurai helmet). The summit of the mound is now home to {{ill|Ochiyama Shrine|ja|冑山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and excavation has not yet been conducted. The Edo-period text {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}} mentions looting of the site during this period.
The mound is circular in shape (some sources suggest a scallop-shaped layout {{Sfn|Kabutoyama Village (Heibonsha)|1993}}) and measures approximately 90 meters in diameter (north-south) with a height of 11.25 meters. This makes it the second-largest circular Kofun in Saitama Prefecture, following the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|qid=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}} in {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|qid=Q860659|lt=Gyōda|1=Gyōda}}.<ref name="ExplanationBoard">Historic site explanation board.</ref> The mound features a two-tiered construction. Fragments of cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been recovered from the mound's surface.<ref name="ExplanationBoard"/> A buried moat likely surrounded the mound, with an estimated total size exceeding 100 meters, including the moat.<ref name="Kumagaya Digital Museum"/>
According to {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}, artifacts unearthed during the looting included armor, clay horse figurines (haniwa), beads, mirrors, and a broken sword, though the exact items are unverified.<ref name="Kumagaya Digital Museum"/>
Kabutoyama Kofun is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} (some sources suggest the early 6th century {{Sfn|Kabutoyama Village (Heibonsha)|1993}}). It lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a prominent ancient burial site in the Hiki Hills.<ref name="Kumagaya Digital Museum"/> Together with the {{ill|Tōkanyama Kofun|ja|とうかん山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkanyama Kofun|qq=}}, located about 1 kilometer to the north in Kumagaya’s Minowa district, Kabutoyama Kofun is considered an important archaeological indicator of regional leadership and governance during the late Kofun period.
The site was designated as a Saitama Prefectural Historic Site in {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} (Heisei 1).<ref name="List"/>
<gallery> File:Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya), enkei-2.jpg|Distant view File:Kabutoyama-jinja (Kumagaya), honden-2.jpg|Shrine hall (designated as a cultural property by Kumagaya City) on the summit File:Kabutoyama-jinja (Kumagaya), haiden.jpg|Worship hall at the base of the mound </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutoyama Kofun - Privately owned. Designated on March 17, 1989.<ref name="List">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/documents/27121/22kensiseki.pdf Saitama Prefectural Historic Sites]}} (Saitama Prefecture website).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{small|Sources not directly cited}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kuraji Nakamura|title=Maruhakayama Kofun and Kabutoyama Kofun - Internal Conflict in the Musashi Kokuzō Clan and Large Circular Kofuns|date=2010|publisher=Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Museum|journal=Sakitama Bulletin|volume=4|pages=1-17|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/page_20200319063440|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōkanyama Kofun|ja|とうかん山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkanyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|qid=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}} * {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kabutoyama Kofun (Kumagaya)}} * [https://www.city.kumagaya.lg.jp/kanko/rekisi/bunkazai/sisekikyuseki/kabutoyama.html Kabutoyama Kofun] - Kumagaya City website * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/037bunka.htm Kabutoyama Kofun] - Kumagaya Municipal Konan Cultural Properties Center "Kumagaya Digital Museum"
{{Kofun navbar}}
